title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 545.430 - Change of venue may be granted defendant
text:
545.430.  Change of venue may be granted defendant. — Any criminal cause pending in any circuit court may be removed, by the order of such court or the judge thereof, to the circuit court of another county in the same circuit, whenever it shall appear, in the manner provided in section 545.490, that the minds of the inhabitants of the county in which the cause is pending are so prejudiced against the defendant that a fair trial cannot be had therein.
(RSMo 1939 § 4015) 
Prior revisions: 1929 § 3626; 1919 § 3969; 1909 § 5176 
Division of county, how pending prosecution conducted, 47.330 
Prosecution begun in wrong county, case to be transferred, procedure, 541.120 to 541.150 
(1952) No change of venue or right to disqualify judge exists in a criminal contempt case. Osborne v. Purdome (Mo.), 250 S.W.2d 159.
